Human impacts
Ari Edmundson
T
In 2011 Braxton-Williams learned the assessor's office had valued
the house at $147,550. "I love my house, but I know it's not worth
that much," she said. "And they know it's not worth that much."
(Terrence Antonio James/Chicago Tribune)
The Chicago Tribune, June 10, 2017

Predicting housing prices at scale ("mass appraisal")
Ari Edmundson
Predict sale price ("fair market value") of unsold properties by discovering
patterns in data sets containing known sale prices and characteristics of
similar and nearby properties.

Social trust and translating accuracy* into fairness
Ari Edmundson
Why should any individual believe that the model is accurate for their property?
The CCAO counts on its performance of "transparency" (putting data, models,
pipeline onto GitLab) to foster public trust, which would help it equate the
production of "accurate assessments" with "fairness"
We often expect the numbers to "speak for themselves" -- but it takes social work to
put them to work
Transparency
Defined as: the ability of the data science department to share and explain pipeline results and decisions to both
internal and external stakeholders
